action.reasoning: Observation: We have ~1.57 hours and 13 iterations left. Best test grounding_score is 0.0089 from the fully fine-tuned merged model, while merged_125 scored 0.0006. The fine-tuned model scores 0.29 on ShowUI-desktop val but fails on ScreenSpot-Pro test, indicating catastrophic forgetting/domain shift. The base model scores 0.0 on val (ShowUI-desktop is OOD), but ScreenSpot-Pro is a standard GUI grounding benchmark that the base model may have seen during pretraining. We have not yet successfully evaluated the base model on test. Analysis: Before burning time on another eval submission, I need to quickly inspect what the base model actually outputs on a few val samples (with and without system prompt) to ensure predict.py's parser can handle its output style and to choose the best prompt for test. Then I will create a proper base-model checkpoint directory and submit it. Decision: Write and run a quick inspection script that loads the base model, runs inference on 5 val samples with/without system prompt, and prints raw outputs. No training.
